Replayed boss from Interlude to try remember what I did <:wisdom:1400771656510734356> 
- AoE would be ideal for clearing back mob or using Tulu spell
- Can save Tulu spell for turns where your shield or weakness app is lacking
- Don't aim for huge burst turns since boss won't go down until all the mobs are dead, try to make it so you can clear at least 1 of em each turn since boss scales
- I cleared it fast with my invested main team <:Feelguilty:1400771618174664775> But I believe at a certain turn Tulu will chime in to help you
- Could potentially tech in Goliath for strength sapping if need be
Oh yeah since you didn't show your whole comp I hope you've equipped your R wheels <:point:1356053112292511815> Especially Gluttony

For F2P don't think of OE until way later on if at all <:Feelguilty:1400771618174664775> The main content D-Tide requires 5 unique teams (no repeated awakeners, wheels, and posses) so you want to go wide and also be able to invest into dupe breakpoints. There's 5 difficulties with A5 being the hardest but that's usually whale/flex category. A4 will require dupes but OE is not needed.

My corpossant is e0

corpo has some niche usages but without *heavy* investment it's quite difficult to a5 on her (unless you gmurphy but that kind of defeats the purpose)

how does thais work?

- Best embryo maker in game
- Can apply vuln or weakness
- Exalt lets you choose one Command Card in hand and duplicate it 2 times
- E2 is recommended because the Exalt reduces the cost of the duplocated cards by 2 Arithmetica
